title: quercus schulzei
intro: Quercus schulzei, also known as the Mexican red oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Quercus genus of the Fagaceae family. Traditionally, the bark of the Quercus schulzei has been used in folk medicine, primarily for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bark is rich in tannins and flavonoids, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, antiseptic, and antiviral activities, as well as helping to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. Quercus schulzei bark can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
